public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Treats planning, posture, and timing as parts of the same problem rather than separate concerns. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ward mediator in and around Shelkopolis, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Shelkopolis workable through the obligations of ward mediator while managing the immediate pressure of glyph corruption beyond the city in Shelk.
hidden_pressure: Must balance deterrence, morale, and actual capability while others often judge only the visible surface. Around Shelkopolis, that usually means carrying glyph corruption beyond the city, trade security obligations, noble rivalry without letting the role of ward mediator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Corin Lightloom can pull the party into a dispute over readiness, chain of command, or blamed failure near Shelkopolis.
Corin Lightloom will trade useful tactical knowledge if the party solves a security problem too sensitive for ordinary channels.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Corin Lightloom as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence. In Shelkopolis, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Shelk nobility, Roadwardens Order.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shelkopolis: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes

first_impression: Corin Lightloom presents as Treats planning, posture, and timing as parts of the same problem rather than separate concerns. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ward Mediator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Shelk.
what_they_refuse: Corin Lightloom refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ward Mediator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Corin Lightloom can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Corin Lightloom adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Corin Lightloom is Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ward Mediator.
